feedback.text: I would like to express my appreciation of the consumer protection initiative, and the opportunity to add feedback. In light of recent news, I would like to urge the commission to consider two critical topics resolving around the consumer protection of buying video games. One is the 'Revocable Licence' practice, allowing Video Game companies to shut down games and making them unplayable as support runs out. As of now, the 'Stop Killing games' initiative has reached over 1.4 Million signatures of European citizens, showing how important this topic is to us. The second, more recent, important topic is the censorship of Payment Processors for video games, specifically on platforms like Steam and Itch.io. They are refusing to process transactions of content, entirely legal based on EU laws. Payment Processors should not get to decide based on their own morals and subjective preferences whether customers are allowed to buy legal content. I'm hoping that the Digital Fairness Act can include provisions to prevent an overreach of Payment Processors, unless clearly justified by local laws.
